title: Cleisostoma paniculatum
taxonomy: Cleisostoma paniculatum is a species of orchid in the genus Cleisostoma. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This orchid species is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its delicate white flowers. The classification of Cleisostoma paniculatum is further divided into the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Coelogyneae. This classification places Cleisostoma paniculatum within the larger orchid family structure.

intro: Cleisostoma paniculatum, also known as the "panicle orchid", is a species of orchid that is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in reducing pain, inflammation, and the risk of infections. The bioactive constituents of Cleisostoma paniculatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleisostoma paniculatum is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and is commonly used to make teas, tonics, and other herbal remedies.
